data.title: SolGold reports Q2 results
data.description: • SolGold press release (OTCPK:SLGGF): Q2 GAAP EPS of -$0.30.

data.title: Sarepta closes on $600M revolving credit facility
data.description:
• Sarepta Therapeutics (NASDAQ:SRPT) has closed on a $600M senior secured revolving credit facility made possible with a bank syndicate.
  • JP Morgan Chase (JPM) served as lead arranger and joint bookrunner.
  • The credit facility "supplements our strong balance sheet and allows for contingent liquidity as we execute our strategic plan to achieve our Sarepta 2030 objectives," CFO Ian Estepan said in a news release.

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P, and Dow are muted, but Wall Street set to snap two-week losing streak
data.description:
The major averages on Friday were little changed, but Wall Street is on track to snap a two-week losing streak. The past few days were dominated by President Donald Trump's remarks on tariffs and by economic data on inflation.

Early on and the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was +0.1%, the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was +0.1%, and the Dow (DJI) was even.

Tariffs took center stage this week, with Trump on Monday announcing 25% levies on all steel and aluminum imports into the U.S. On Thursday, Trump signed a memorandum that tasked his administration to investigate current trade relations and propose reciprocal tariffs on a country-specific basis.

Still, the fact that Trump did not immediately put in effect the tit-for-tat tariffs and was scant on details, helped market sentiment and drove Wall Street more than 1% higher in Thursday's session. A favorable producer inflation report for January also helped.

On the surface, the data showed that the M/M increase in the headline producer price index (PPI) was higher than expectations, while the core PPI came in-line. However, digging deeper, the components in the PPI that directly feed into the core personal consumption expenditures (PCE) price index came in soft for January. That bodes well for the Federal Reserve's favorite inflation gauge.

The PPI update helped overcome the shock provided by hotter-than-expected consumer price index (CPI) readings on Wednesday.

"It was a love-in for markets yesterday with bonds and equities rallying following better-than-expected inflation data and news that details on reciprocal tariffs would not come before April. This led to a revival of hopes that the Fed would still cut rates this year, particularly after the more hawkish fears post the upside surprise in the CPI report the previous day," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

Treasury yields slipped on Friday after January retail sales came in significantly worse than expected. The 10-year yield (US10Y) dipped 6 basis points to 4.47%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) was also down 6 basis points to 4.26%.

After the closing bell, 13F filings will be in focus - regulatory disclosures by major funds of their quarterly equity ownership changes.

data.title: Norsk Hydro closed four extrusion plants in 2024 on weak EV demand
data.description:
Norsk Hydro (OTCQX:NHYDY) closed four extrusion plants in the U.S., U.K., France and Lithuania to reduce costs in the unit that was affected by weak electric vehicle sales, the aluminum producer said after it posted a Q4 profit miss on Friday.

The closures, which were carried out throughout 2024 and led to a cut of more than 900 full-time jobs, are not related to U.S. tariffs, CEO Eivind Kallevik told Reuters.

However, "as the U.S. is a major aluminum importer, tariffs will likely raise premiums and costs, with Midwest premiums already surging to the highest levels since April 2023," Norsk Hydro (OTCQX:NHYDY) said in its earnings statement.

Norsk Hydro (OTCQX:NHYDY) said the extrusions business is little affected by the tariffs, since it relies mainly on domestic raw materials and typically passes higher LME prices and premiums on to customers; the unit, which makes aluminum extrusions used in auto production, is instead suffering from weak EV demand, especially in Germany.

Norsk Hydro (OTCQX:NHYDY) reported Q4 adjusted EBITDA more than doubled to 7.7B Norwegian crowns (~$690M) but missed the 8.28B crowns analyst consensus estimate.

The company said it expects underlying demand for its products to gradually improve into 2025 due to lower interest rates.

data.description:

data.title: Informatica slumps after rocky Q4 sparks wave of downgrades
data.description:
Informatica (NYSE:INFA) shares tumbled around 35% premarket on Friday after the data management company was hit with a string of downgrades following its weak quarterly results.

Baird downgraded Informatica (NYSE:INFA) to Neutral, citing weaker trends and reduced visibility. Informatica's Q4 results highlighted a deceleration in cloud ARR growth, the brokerage said, with cloud subscription ARR up 34.1% Y/Y to $827.3M vs. 36.1% growth in Q3. The company now forecasts Q1 cloud subscription ARR growth of 29.6% and 25.1% for the full year 2025 vs. Baird’s prior 31.0% full-year estimate.

Operational challenges in renewals and accounting adjustments for cloud migrations also impacted short-term results. Q4 revenue fell 3.8% Y/Y to $428.3M, missing analysts’ consensus estimates of $28.74M.

“INFA reported weaker Q4 results, driven by weaker renewals and higher (partial) churn across the company's on-premise and cloud segments. The weaker trends also negatively impacted 2025 revenue and profitability guidance, including cloud ARR growth, the central focus of our prior positive thesis," Baird said.

RBC Capital Markets downgraded Informatica (INFA) to Sector Perform from Outperform, while lowering its price target from $35 to $19.

“INFA reported a messy/confusing qtr where results missed expectations partially due to sales execution issues around cloud renewals as we struggle to clearly understand what really went wrong. While estimates could be de-risked and the LT opportunity still looks interesting, we believe it could take multiple quarters of improved execution to regain investor confidence,” RBC analysts said in a note.

Informatica (INFA) has also been cut to Equal-Weight at Wells Fargo, while Scotiabank, Truist, Cantor and UBS cut their price targets on the stock to $27, $34, $29 and $30, respectively.

INFA shares were down over 34% premarket on Friday to $16.59.

data.title: AMC Networks misses on main metrics in Q4 as costs swell
data.description:
AMC Networks (NASDAQ:AMCX) reported a top-and-bottom line miss for its fourth quarter results on Friday.

The company said subscription revenues dipped 4% to $314M due to declines in the linear subscriber universe, while streaming revenues rose 8% to $156M from last year.

Free cash flow for the company fell to $37.6M in Q4 from $66M for the same period last year, while operating loss swelled to $254.2M from a loss of $11M last year.

AMC recorded more than $345M in impairment and restructuring charges for Q4. Adjusted operating income was $129M, with a margin of 22%.

For the three months ended December 31, the company reported a net loss of $284.5M, or $6.38 loss per share, up from a loss of $21.8M, or $0.50 loss per share, for the same period last year.

On an adjusted basis, it earned $64 per share, missing the average Wall Street analyst expectation of $1.05 apiece.

Revenue fell nearly 12% to $599.3M and also missed estimates.

Shares of the company rose nearly 7% in premarket trading and pared most gains before the opening bell.

data.title: U.S. industrial production advances more than expected in January
data.description: U.S. industrial production grew 0.5% M/M in January, outpacing the +0.3% consensus and trailing December's +1.0%, according to data released by the Federal Reserve on Friday.

data.title: Eutelsat targets €500M-€600M CapEx for 2025 with focus on LEO expansion
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Eutelsat Group (OTCPK:EUTLF) Q2 2025

Management View

  • CEO Eva Berneke highlighted the signing of the agreement for the SpaceRISE consortium to design, build, and operate the Iris2 multi-orbit constellation, marking a significant step in Eutelsat's LEO strategy. The company procured 100 LEO satellites to support the constellation and anticipates a total of 340 additional satellites over the medium term.

  • Financial results for the first half of 2024-2025 showed operating vertical revenues of €600 million, a 3.9% increase on a like-for-like basis. Adjusted EBITDA margin was stable at 55.2%, with gross CapEx now expected to be between €500 million and €600 million, down from the initial guidance of €700 million to €800 million. A goodwill impairment of €535 million was recognized for GEO assets due to lower expected future cash flows.

  • CFO Christophe Caudrelier detailed segmental performance, noting Video revenues declined by 6.4% to €309 million, while Fixed Connectivity and Government Services both rose by 22% to €119 million and €96 million, respectively. Mobile Connectivity increased by 7% to €75 million.

Outlook

  • CEO Berneke confirmed the company’s financial objectives for fiscal year 2024-2025, with revenues expected to remain flat year-on-year and adjusted EBITDA margin slightly below 2024 levels. The company is targeting a medium-term leverage of 3 times.

  • The Iris2 project, valued at €10.6 billion, is expected to generate €6.5 billion in revenues over a 12-year concession period. Eutelsat’s investment in the project will be back-end loaded, starting in 2028.

  • The sale and leaseback of passive ground infrastructure is expected to generate €500 million in net proceeds by the first half of calendar year 2026.

Financial Results

  • Total revenues for the first half of fiscal 2024-2025 reached €606 million, a 5.9% increase on a reported basis. Adjusted EBITDA stood at €334.9 million, compared to €365.6 million a year earlier, with a net debt-to-adjusted EBITDA ratio of 3.92.

  • The backlog at the end of December 2024 was €3.7 billion, with connectivity now representing 56% of the total. LEO accounted for 48% of this segment.

  • Operating costs increased by €64.3 million year-on-year, reflecting the impact of the OneWeb consolidation and cost control measures.

Q&A

  • Roshan Ranjit, Deutsche Bank: Asked about GEO headwinds and LEO’s capacity to offset declines. CEO Berneke noted significant B2C headwinds driven by Starlink and the repurposing of KONNECT VHTS capacity for Mobility markets.

  • Mark Watts, Citi: Inquired about the ground terminal proceeds and lease costs. CFO Caudrelier estimated annual costs at €70-€80 million, mostly accounted as OpEx.

  • Sami Kassab, BNP Paribas: Queried Iris2’s financial commitments and returns. CEO Berneke stated a minimum IRR of 10%-12% is expected. She also explained that GEO broadband headwinds are temporary, pending renegotiations with a KONNECT VHTS customer.

  • Ben Rickett, New Street Research: Asked about OneWeb’s revenue ramp-up. CEO Berneke highlighted a progressive ramp-up with no significant jumps expected.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed concerns over GEO headwinds and the competitive threat from Starlink. The tone was slightly negative, with questions focusing on potential growth offsets and financial impacts.

  • Management maintained a slightly positive tone, emphasizing strategic advances in LEO and Iris2 and reiterating confidence in medium-term objectives. However, defensive statements such as "we are looking at alternative solutions" indicated caution.

  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone showed increased vigilance, reflecting heightened focus on GEO challenges and cost controls.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance was revised downward for CapEx, now expected at €500-€600 million, compared to the €700-€800 million range in the previous quarter.

  • Strategic focus shifted more prominently to LEO expansion and Iris2, while GEO investments were scaled back amid market challenges.

  • Analysts' tone remained consistent quarter-over-quarter, with skepticism regarding GEO's performance and LEO’s competitive positioning.

  • Management demonstrated increased confidence in Iris2 and partnerships for long-term growth compared to prior discussions.

Risks and Concerns

  • The €535 million goodwill impairment on GEO assets reflects lower expected cash flows and increased competition in the connectivity market.

  • Analysts raised concerns over Starlink’s growing influence on both B2C and B2B markets, particularly in Mobility and broadband.

  • Regulatory delays in key markets like India and challenges in finalizing LEO ground infrastructure were highlighted.

Final Takeaway

Eutelsat emphasized its strategic pivot toward LEO and Iris2, reducing GEO investments amid market headwinds. While financial results met expectations, the €535 million GEO impairment and competitive pressures from Starlink remain key challenges. The company’s focus on cost control, partnerships, and LEO expansion underpins its confidence in medium-term growth prospects.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: GoDaddy tumbles after mixed Q4, but analysts keep bullish views
data.description:
Shares of GoDaddy (NYSE:GDDY) fell about 9% premarket on Friday after mixed fourth-quarter results, while analysts were largely bullish.

Benchmark reiterated its Buy rating on GoDaddy and raised the price target on the stock to $275 from $230.

Analysts led by Mark Zgutowicz said they were taking 2025 estimates for revenue up +0.5% to $4.919B, or -0.4% below the high end of guidance, reflecting a -1%/+1% revision to Applications & Commerce, or A&C, and Core Platform, or CP, respectively.

The analysts expected first quarter/2025 estimates of high-teens A&C revenue growth year-over-year given 20%+ 2024 bookings growth (versus guidance of mid-teens), however, they said that they understand product mix including transaction-related bookings recognized intra-quarter, creates an optical booking/revenue growth mismatch.

Regarding GoDaddy's average revenue per user, or ARPU, growth opportunity, Zgutowicz and his team said that fourth quarter average order size increased +16% year-over-year, with incremental, highly accretive new product levers at its disposal in the next 12 months, or NTM, e.g., Digital Marketing Suite and GoDaddy Capital.

The analysts noted that GoDaddy’s revenue guidance range reflects conservative transaction-based revenue assumptions, mainly concentrated within domain Aftermarket (tough comps) given the lack of visibility into large deal demand trends in the next 12 months.

Meanwhile, Citi said GoDaddy reported a strong beat in the fourth quarter (something which is becoming more regular) with initial 2025 guidance it views as conservative and having solid upside tension.

The analysts said the stock declining after the quarterly report is not representative of the strength of the results.

Citi raised their 2025 revenue, Normalised EBITDA, and free cash flow, or FCF, estimates and sees further upside as the year goes on.

The analysts noted that GoDaddy's management has been guiding conservatively, with the top end of the revenue range at +8% year-over-year, and commentary that bookings will again grow faster than revenue, the analysts see continued momentum.

The product execution is working. GoDaddy Airo/GenAl continues to gain traction, customers are attaching more products and increasing their spending, and management expects material contribution again in 2025 from Pricing and Bundling initiatives, now coming across both A&C and CP segments, the analysts added.

Related stocks: Shares of Wix.com (WIX) were largely flat premarket on Friday.
